tagline: Help Isobel reach the World Scout Jamboree
heroDescription: In summer 2027, I have the opportunity to represent Nottinghamshire and the UK at the 26th World Scout Jamboree in Poland. I am fundraising to help make this amazing adventure possible.

paragraphs: Hi, I'm Isobel (but my friends call me Issy)! I am 15 years old and I've been a Scout for many years. I am honoured to have been selected to represent Nottinghamshire and the UK at the 26th World Scout Jamboree in 2027 in Poland—a global celebration of unity, adventure, and discovery among young people.

Being in Scouts has shaped my confidence, resilience, and curiosity. I hope to grow from this experience, share what I learn, and be a positive role model for the Cubs I support as a Young Leader, and for younger members of Scouting beginning their own journeys.

Beyond Explorers, I volunteer in my community, including helping cater for a recent minis rugby tour at my local club and supporting local sports events. Giving back is important to me, and the Jamboree is another opportunity to live those values on a global stage.

To make this opportunity possible, I aim to raise £3,500. My fundraising will include tackling the Marathon Walk Challenge, running cake stalls, selling mystery books and handmade candles, and offering special Mother’s Day gift boxes. Every contribution brings me one step closer to Poland—and to a journey that represents both personal growth and the spirit of adventure and service at the heart of Scouting. Thank you sincerely for your support!

jamboreeInfo.description: The World Scout Jamboree is an educational event that brings together 50,000 young people from all corners of the globe. The 2027 theme, 'Bravely', urges young people to act courageously in building a sustainable future, bridging cultural differences, and tackling global challenges together.

scoutingImpact.title: Confidence
scoutingImpact.desc: Tackling high ropes, leading night hikes, and speaking up in patrol forums has helped me grow and trust my voice.

scoutingImpact.title: Teamwork
scoutingImpact.desc: Whether putting up tents in a storm or resolving camp chores, I've learned how to coordinate and cooperate with anyone.

scoutingImpact.title: Independence
scoutingImpact.desc: Being away from home, managing my own gear, and organizing my budget makes me feel ready for any adventure.

scoutingImpact.title: Leadership
scoutingImpact.desc: As a Young Leader, I help teach outdoor skills, navigation, and activities to younger Cub Scouts in our unit.

scoutingImpact.title: Resilience
scoutingImpact.desc: Getting damp in rainstorms or losing the path temporarily has taught me to laugh, keep going, and find positive solutions.

scoutingImpact.title: Belonging
scoutingImpact.desc: I've made lifelong friends from different backgrounds who support each other. It feels like a big, welcoming global family.

activities.title: Yorkshire Three Peaks Challenge
activities.desc: Tackling the demanding Yorkshire Three Peaks hike (24 miles, 5,200ft of ascent) in under 12 hours to raise sponsorships.

activities.title: Catering for Minis Rugby Tour
activities.desc: Volunteered and helped cater for the minis rugby tour at my local rugby club, donating proceeds to my Jamboree fund.

activities.title: Mother's Day Gift Boxes
activities.desc: Hand-crafted and curated custom gift boxes for Mother's Day, sold to supportive neighbors and friends.

activities.title: Mystery Books & Handmade Candles
activities.desc: Creating scented hand-poured candles and wrapping mystery books with short clues to sell at local school and craft fairs.

updates.title: First UK Contingent Training Weekend!
updates.desc: Just returned from my first training weekend with our regional unit! We practiced team building, pitched the large patrol tents, and started planning our unit identity. It was amazing to meet the other 35 Scouts who I'll be travelling with.

updates.title: Launch of my Fundraising Website
updates.desc: My dad and I built this website to share my journey! I am super excited to keep you all updated on my progress. The GoFundMe link is live and secure, and thank you for visiting!

safeguarding.notice: For Isobel's safety, this website is managed and monitored by her parents. No personal contact details, precise timetables, or real-time location tracking are published here. All messages sent through the contact form are routed directly to her parents.
